title: Worker that handles a large map of arguments safely
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.DocumentProcessor` (queue `:documents`, max_attempts 5). perform/1 accepts a nested args map: `%{"doc_id" => id, "metadata" => %{"title" => title, "tags" => tags, "updated_at" => updated_at_iso}}`. Extract each field with string keys. Parse `updated_at_iso` with `DateTime.from_iso8601/1` and handle both success and error cases (return `{:cancel, :invalid_date}` on parse failure). Tags is a list of strings; validate all members are strings with `Enum.all?/2` and return `{:cancel, :invalid_tags}` if not. Then call `MyApp.Documents.process(id, title, tags, updated_at)`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/document_processor.ex`.
tier_rationale: Nested string-keyed destructuring + multi-branch validation + ISO datetime parse. Many moving parts.
